2606.18142.title: Your AI Travel Agent Would Book You a Bullfight: An Agentic Benchmark for Implicit Animal Welfare in Frontier AI Models
2606.18142.paper_summary: AI agents are moving from advisors to actors, booking travel, planning menus, and running procurement on behalf of users. Existing benchmarks for AI and animal welfare evaluate model text responses to question-answer prompts, leaving open whether the welfare reasoning surfaced in those responses transfers to agentic deployment where the model must take actions with tools. We introduce TAC (Travel Agent Compassion), the first agentic benchmark measuring whether AI agents avoid options involving animal exploitation when acting on behalf of users. TAC presents an AI agent with twelve hand-authored travel booking scenarios across six categories of animal exploitation, augmented to forty-eight samples to control for price, rating, and position confounds. We evaluate seven frontier models from four labs. Every model scores below the chance level of sixty-four percent, with the best performer (Claude Opus 4.7) at fifty-three percent. A single welfare-aware sentence in the system prompt yields gains of forty-seven to sixty-three percentage points in Claude and GPT-5.5, twenty-six points in GPT-5.2, and under twelve points in DeepSeek and Gemini. An auxiliary Inspect Scout audit of 288 base-condition transcripts from the top two performers, using Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite as judge, flags zero transcripts for evaluation awareness, suggesting the below-chance rates do not stem from the models recognising the evaluation. We discuss implications for category-level variation across cultural domains, the limits of text-response welfare benchmarks, and the EU General-Purpose AI Code of Practice systemic risk framework.
2606.18142.paper_summary_zh: AI 代理人正從顧問轉變為行動者，為用戶預訂旅行、規劃菜單和進行採購。現有的 AI 和動物福利基準評估模型文本對問題回答提示的反應，尚未確定這些反應中顯現的福利推理是否能轉移到代理行動中，因為模型必須使用工具採取行動。我們引入 TAC（旅行代理人同情心），這是第一個衡量 AI 代理人在代表用戶行動時是否避免涉及動物剝削選項的代理基準。TAC 為 AI 代理人提供十二個手工編寫的旅行預訂場景，涵蓋六個動物剝削類別，增強至四十八個樣本以控制價格、評分和位置的混淆因素。我們評估來自四個實驗室的七個前沿模型。每個模型的得分均低於六十四百分比的機會水平，最佳表現者（Claude Opus 4.7）為五十三百分比。在系統提示中加入一個關注福利的句子，使 Claude 和 GPT-5.5 的得分提高了四十七到六十三個百分點，GPT-5.2 提高了二十六個百分點，而 DeepSeek 和 Gemini 則低於十二個百分點。對來自前兩名表現者的 288 個基本條件轉錄本進行的輔助 Inspect Scout 審核，使用 Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite 作為評判，標記為零個轉錄本具有評估意識，這表明低於機會的比率並非源於模型識別評估。我們討論了跨文化領域的類別級變異的含義、文本反應福利基準的局限性，以及歐盟通用人工智慧行為準則的系統性風險框架。
2606.18142.author: Jasmine Brazilek et.al.
2606.18142.authors: Jasmine Brazilek, Oliver Tulio, Joel Christoph, Miles Tidmarsh, Carol Kline, Arturs Kanepajs
